Blackberry brambles run alongside a brick wall in the side parking lot of a small Vietnamese cafe or something. Watch out for huge ants though!
Huge fig tree in front of Cherokee Arms Apartments- REALLY tall tree along brick fence. Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: July-unknown.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Pear tree in front yard. High yielding. Ask owner's permission before picking. Access: Private but overhanging.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Heirloom plums and apples on the east side of the Magevney House lawn Access: Public.
Tall pecan tree in backyard. Ask homeowner for access. Access: Added by owner.
Falling Fruit access: Added by owner.
Small black walnut tree. While on Millington road cross N. Watkins the take first left. 100-200 ft. down, on left in fence. Only tree in fence. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: October-November.
Brambles under large tree where there has been some erosion where the grass hill meets the overpass fence.
Brambles along yard line Access: Private.

Blackberry brambles alongside the right of the stairs from the overlook (if looking at the river). Area was recently were mowed, but I think they will grow back just as lush again this summer! Access: Public.

Brambles running all along the southwest edge of the walkway. Falling Fruit season: April-July.

There are wild blackerries growing all along the bank of the river. You can access them from inside the park by just going over the hill or park in the parking lot and head towards the river bank. You'll see them! Falling Fruit season: May-June.
